sources.full: Drop In Dungeons: Sanctum of Ill Omens

book.name: Drop In Dungeons: Sanctum of Ill Omens

contents.name: Chapter 1: Introduction
headers: Welcome to the Sanctum of Ill Omens

entries.name: Welcome to the Sanctum of Ill Omens
entries: Thank you for going on this adventure with us at Leopard Print Press! The Sanctum of Ill Omens presents an easy-to-use dungeon that can be dropped into any existing or upcoming campaign world.

Due to its location on a secret demiplane, the sanctum is not tied to any specific location. As always, you can have it appear in any space you wish it to! Discovering how to travel to the Sanctum can be an adventure in itself.
Inside this sanctum are 5 encounters. One is a puzzle/battle and the other 4 are all roleplay encounters that can become battles depending on how the party acts.
{@b This dungeon is designed for parties between levels 5-8.}

entries.name: Suggestions On How To Use This Dungeon
entries: Below are some suggestions on how to integrate this dungeon into your game world.

entries.name: Reveal Backstory and Plot Elements
entries: The Sanctum used to belong to a divination wizard, so like its namesake, it can be used to introduce fortune telling and prophecy to your existing campaign.
Revealing character or world backstory, information on the BBEG, or the location of a needed magic item are all within the realm of possibility with this dungeon.

entries.name: Introduce a Warlock Patron
entries: Within the Sanctum lies the Cambion Mizzark, who has been trapped and left within the Sanctum for centuries. He is willing to do whatever it takes to escape and can be a potential Warlock Patron for any player characters willing to make a dark pact.

entries.name: Death of a PC or NPC
entries: The Cloning Chamber (Floor 3) can be the location where a wayward soul known to the party has traveled and begun inhabiting one of the clones of Giselle the Fatebound.

entries.name: Introduce Spells
entries: The spellbooks and scrolls within are a great way to give your characters access to new magic and build their repertoire. There are over 25 spells that can be attained from this dungeon.

entries.name: Base of Operations
entries: Once the party gains control of the sanctum, it can be used as a base of operations. There is a teleportation circle that can be used to travel in and out of the sanctum should a party member have the magical ability to do so. The party can also use the {@b {@item Nostradam Mirror|LPPDiDSoIO}} to help with their adventures.

entries.name: Bind Extraplanar Creatures
entries: The Summoning Room (Floor 2) contains amplified versions of {@spell Planar Binding|PHB} and {@spell Magic Circle|PHB} spells. Enterprising players can use these devices to bind powerful creatures to their will.

entries.name: As an Addition to Drop In Villains and Factions
entries: This tower ties into our previous supplement, {@link Drop In Villains and Factions|https://www.drivethrurpg.com/it/product/479704/drop-in-villains-and-factions}. The tower belonged to one of the villains, {@i Giselle Fatebound}, and can be used as part of her overarching plot. She has been trapped within her Gem Prison for centuries and needs the grimoire located within to help free her from entrapment.

Should she accompany the party on this visit, she can help with the traps and give information on the contents within the tower, but her memory may miss some things. She will also dodge questions when it is revealed within the tower that she was responsible for the failed {@b Ascension Ritual}, a spell that would rewrite reality and sever the god's control over the realms. This failed ritual caused a near apocalypse in the past and can be used as part of your worldbuilding to explain a past natural disaster or apocalyptic event.

entries.name: Sanctum Backstory
entries: Hundreds of years ago, this tower and its plane were created by the Archmage Giselle the Fatebound. Swirling purple and green energy storms rage as a testament to her once great power. The large tower, once the seat of her arcane mastery and ambitions, now lies abandoned. The wizard's research into divination and fortune telling were prominent in her time, but rumors swirl that she was also involved in dark summoning rituals.

The tower's corridors whisper of hidden knowledge, vast treasures, and powerful magical artifacts. Daring adventurers beware - while the tower's owner has left, the spells and defenses of the tower have not.

entries.name: Adventure Hooks
entries: Use any of these hooks in order to send your adventurers to The Sanctum of Ill Omens!

entries.name: Arcane Prisoner
entries: Giselle the Fatebound has been trapped in a magical gem prison, which has been recovered by the party. She telepathically gives them the location of a portal that will take the party to the tower. She needs a grimoire located in the sanctum to gain her freedom and promises the party all of the treasures within if they can help her retrieve the grimoire.

entries.name: Echoes of Prophecy
entries: The party hears of a powerful divining mirror at the top of the abandoned tower that can tell them vital information about their quest. The Nostradam Mirror as it appears in texts is also rumored to be able to tell the future and gain information on nearly any entity.

entries.name: Power Struggle
entries: A powerful wizard has gained knowledge about the abandoned tower and seeks an artifact within. This wizard needs a party of adventurers to find the tower and disarm its defenses. He offers whatever treasures lie within as long as he gets the artifact - the Nostradam Mirror.

entries.name: Wayward Apprentice
entries: A promising young wizard has disappeared from their studies, leaving behind a note stating that they have discovered a location filled with great magics. The wizarding academy is offering a reward for the apprentice's safe return.

entries.name: A Misplaced Soul
entries: A beloved NPC, a family member, or a close friend recently died. However, something bizarre happened upon their death - their soul did not go to the afterlife, and they are unable to be communicated with via {@spell Speak With Dead|PHB} spells or brought back to life via resurrection-like spells or abilities.
A cleric or wizard notifies the party that they know where the soul went - into the Sanctum of Ill Omens located on a hidden demiplane.

entries.name: The Dark Pact
entries: The party has learned about a demonic prisoner being held within this tower. It must be interrogated, slain, or freed.

entries.title: Map of the Bridge to the tower with Details
entries.altText: Map of the Bridge to the tower with Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Bridge to the tower with Details

entries.title: Map of the Bridge to the tower without Details
entries.altText: Map of the Bridge to the tower without Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Bridge to the tower without Details

entries.name: Description
entries: {@i Through the swirling, empty landscape, you see the Sanctum of Ill Omens jutting out of the plane's endless night sky. Purple energy swirls around the top of the tower, a sign of the magic still within. A stone bridge leads to the tower's entrance, crossing over a deep abyss below. Glowing green cracks appear in the bridge's stonework, humming with magical energy. Two towers stand guard over the bridge, etched with red and green runes. It appears as if these towers are waiting for you to approach so that they can release the power stored within. Two large, armored golems stand in the middle of the bridge, unmoving.}
The bridge is 150 feet off the ground.

entries.name: Bridge Defenses
entries: The bridge boasts many defenses meant to keep out interlopers. The defenses can be disarmed by entering the correct password into the Runic Console. However, some of the defenses may still stay armed due to the console failing in its old age.

entries.name: Runic Console
entries: This console controls the traps on the bridge. The runes are written in Infernal. A successful DC 14 Arcana Check can also translate the writing on the console.
Four runes at the top of the console glow, representing which tower defenses are active.

items: {@b Red Rune} - Represents the Fireball Defenses
{@b Green Rune} - Represents the Acid Arrow Defenses
{@b Blue Rune} - Represents the Armored Golem Defenses
{@b Yellow Rune} - Represents the Catapult Trap Defenses

entries: Underneath the runes is a riddle, stating:
"{@i Always coming, but never arrives Makes young lovers husbands and wives While we are waiting, it makes up our lives Something for which, man constantly strives}"
Below the riddle are runes representing the infernal alphabet. When an incorrect entry is entered, the console will temporarily light up in red before resetting itself to its default state.
Entering "{@b future}" into the runic console will solve the puzzle.
Due to the console's age, entering the correct password may not disable all of the Bridge Defenses. When a trap is disabled, the rune representing it will stop glowing.
Roll a d6 and determine which trap remains functioning despite the puzzle being solved.

rows: Acid Arrow Defense Tower remains active

entries: {@b If Giselle The Fatebound is with the party}, she does not remember the answer to the riddle. " {@i I would just teleport into my tower. Why would I ever use this bridge ?}"

entries.name: Fireball Defense Tower
entries: At the end of the walkway are two towers, each boasting a massive, glowing rune on the side of the tower that faces the bridge.

{@b An Arcana Check DC 10} will reveal that this rune shoots fireballs at any object or person who enters the bridge. Should the players throw a rock at the bridge, for example, the turret will fire at that rock.

{@b On Initiative Count 1}, the Fireball Defense Tower shoots one {@spell fireball|level=3|PHB} (at 3rd level) centered on a creature or object that has entered the bridge. Each creature within a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on the point of contact must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. A target takes 8d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
{@b Rune Malfunction:} Roll a 1d20. On a 5 or lower, the Fireball Defense Tower fails to shoot.

entries.name: Acid Arrow Defense Tower
entries: This tower has a glowing green rune, which acts in a similar manner as the Fireball Rune. However, this one will shoot {@spell Melf's Acid Arrow|PHB|Acid Arrow} instead. This rune can also target flying creatures who approach the tower whereas the Fireball Rune only attacks objects or creatures on the bridge. This is to prevent creatures that can fly from avoiding the bridge entirely.

{@b An Arcana check DC 10} will reveal that this rune shoots {@spell Melf's Acid Arrow|PHB|Acid Arrows} at any object or person who enters the bridge or the airspace near the tower. Should the players throw a rock over the bridge, for example, the turret will fire at that rock.

{@b On Initiative Count 1}, the Acid Arrow Defense Tower shoots one acid arrow (at 3rd level) centered on a creature or object that has entered the bridge or is over or in the chasm leading to the tower. A shimmering green arrow streaks towards the target and bursts in a spray of acid. A target must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 4d4} acid damage immediately and {@damage 2d4} acid damage at the end of its next turn. On a miss, the arrow splashes the target with acid for half as much of the initial damage, and then does no damage at the end of the target creature or object's next turn.
{@b Rune Malfunction:} Roll a {@dice 1d20}. On a 5 or lower, the Acid Arrow Rune fails to shoot.

entries.name: Catapult Traps
entries: When a creature enters the area denoted by these magical traps, they are forcefully flung into the air perpendicular to the bridge 30 feet from their original position and off the side of the bridge, resulting in a 150 foot fall.

{@b On a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw}, the creature successfully dodges the trap and suffers no effects. Should a creature fall victim to a catapult trap, they can successfully grab the ledge of the bridge on a DC 12 Athletics or Acrobatics saving throw, thus avoiding the 150 foot fall.
{@b Perception or Investigation 12} will spot the traps. Detect magic will also show the traps, which glow with transmutation magic.
{@b Disarming the Traps}: Dispel Magic or entering the password on the Runic Console will disarm these traps.
{@b Trap Malfunction}: Roll a 1d20 after a creature enters the area of the trap. On a 5 or lower, the trap malfunctions and stops working.

entries.name: Guardian Golems
entries: Two guardian golems guard the bridge. When one steps on the bridge, the lights on their eyes begin to glow a bright blue. They will attack any who enter the bridge. The golems will also avoid the catapult traps and may actively attempt to grapple and move creatures onto the catapult traps. Their Golem Halberd attack also moves enemies 5 feet, which they can use to push enemies off the bridge or into catapult traps.

{@b DM Note:} The golems have damage resistances to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing, so if your party does not have access to magical weapons at this point, it may be prudent to remove the damage resistances from the golems as mentioned in the the "Lowering Difficulty" section.

entries.name: Extra Challenge
entries: To further challenge your players, you can add the following abilities to the Golems:

items: When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack, the creature's speed becomes 0 for the rest of the turn.
Creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you even if they take the Disengage action before leaving your reach.

When a creature within 5 feet of you makes an attack against a target other than you (and that target doesn't have this feat), you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against the attacking creature.

When you take the Attack action and attack with only a glaive, halberd, quarterstaff, or spear, you can use a bonus action to make a melee attack with the opposite end of the weapon. This attack uses the same ability modifier as the primary attack. The weapon's damage die for this attack is a d4, and it deals bludgeoning damage.
While you are wielding a glaive, halberd, pike, quarterstaff, or spear, other creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they enter the reach you have with that weapon.

entries.title: Map of the First Floor - Living Room with Details
entries.altText: Map of the First Floor - Living Room with Details
entries.mapName: Map of the First Floor - Living Room with Details

entries.title: Map of the First Floor - Living Room without Details
entries.altText: Map of the First Floor - Living Room without Details
entries.mapName: Map of the First Floor - Living Room without Details

entries.name: Description
entries: {@i Through the heavy doors, you are met with a room of pristine elegance. The stone floor boasts various carpets and exotic rugs. Multicolored and slightly faded tapestries adorn the walls. Comfortable and stylish, this looks to be a floor for leisure. Bookshelves line the back half of the floor, each filled with volumes of well cared for hardcover books.}

{@i An assortment of comfortable couches and a coffee table sits nearby, enticing you to sit and rest. To your right lies what looks to be a magical elevator. An empty socket sits by the door of the elevator - the rune to operate it looks like it is missing.}
{@i A sweet voice rings out from the desk in front of you.} "{@i Greetings, travelers. Are you guests of Lady Fatebound ?}"

entries.name: Tarot, The Clay Golem Maid
entries: {@i A beautiful, intricately-dressed figure smiles as you approach. She stands at around 7 feet tall, is clad in wizard robes, and wields a magic staff. Her smooth skin appears to be sculpted from enchanted clay. Lines run along her skin like tattoos, thrumming with magical energy and giving her an otherworldly glow.}
{@i The shiny material of her robe glimmers when she moves. Two polished gemstones glow softly in place of eyes, decorating her serene and elegant face.}

entries.name: Background
entries: Tarot is Giselle's sentient clay golem and keeper of the tower, which puts her in possession of {@b The Elevator Rune}. Her programming states that she is to maintain the tower's inner chambers to the best of her ability and to attack intruders should they mean harm to the tower's occupants.
The tower's occupants are defined as herself (Tarot), the Wizard who lived there (Giselle Fatebound), and the occupants of the 3rd floor.

Tarot does not know that Gisele has a room full of clones on the 3rd floor - only that she must protect that floor's occupants at all costs. Her role as the tower's maid also involves greeting guests and making sure they are treated well.

For centuries, Tarot has kept the first, second, and fourth floors of the tower in good condition. Eventually, she grew bored of her tasks and began to read the books within the tower. Before long, she memorized every line from the books within the tower's library. After finding her mistress's spellbooks, Tarot taught herself some of the spells within and has become an accomplished spellcaster on her own. She now dresses in Giselle's clothes, eager to replicate the characters found in the books she has read.

Prior to the party's visit, Tarot used the Nostradam Mirror to tell her future. It gave her terrifying visions along with some visions of hope, which will affect how she views the party and the tower's occupants.

entries.name: Behavior
entries: Tarot is kind hearted but wary of the party initially. Her programming dictates that she is courteous to guests, but she must also ensure that they are not a threat. Should the party introduce themselves as guests, her demeanor will be friendly. Should the party be hostile or get caught sneaking in, she will be aggressive but willing to negotiate. Tarot believes that her master is still alive somewhere in the planes and will never refer to Giselle in the past tense. Should Giselle be accompanying the party, they will have a heartfelt reunion.

A friendly Tarot will warn the party about the 2nd and 3rd floor, stating that the 2nd floor's devil occupant terrifies her. The 3rd floor is off limits, and the 4th floor is her mistress's bedroom. She no longer visits the 2nd floor because of her fear of Mizzark, the Cambion that Giselle imprisoned years ago.
Tarot has not been allowed to visit the 3rd floor, but has heard a strange noise coming from it and now wants to explore it with the party protecting her.
If questioned more deeply about the nature of each floor, Tarot will explain the following:

items: 1st floor - Where the party is now. Tarot will tell the party to make themselves at home and that they are free to peruse any of the materials in the library.

2nd floor - The summoning room. Tarot's master, Giselle Fatebound, would summon and bind various demons, devils, elementals, and fey to her will. It also acts as a prison. Tarot is too afraid to go into that floor now as she is terrified of one of the prisoners - Mizzark.

3rd floor - She does not know. The room is off limits, but she was told by Giselle to protect those within it at all costs, even if it meant dying. Tarot is immensely curious about what is within and now wants to explore the floor.
4th floor - Giselle's bedroom, which is off limits to guests. She will make an exception for the party, however, should they help her deal with Mizzark and find out what is wrong with the 3rd floor.

entries.name: If Asked About the Nostradam Mirror
entries: Tarot will excitedly explain the mirror's powers, which include divination and telling the future. The mirror is how Giselle would do most of her research, looking into possible futures and gaining information needed for her great works.

If asked about these great works, Tarot will refuse to answer, only stating that it will “save us all.” Tarot will tell the party that the mirror is on the 4th floor, but the orb that allows it to work is on the 3rd floor.

entries.name: Operating The Elevator
entries: Tarot possesses the rune that will allow the elevator to work. She will agree to operate the elevator for the party under the following conditions:
{@b Condition 1}: The party assists her with dealing with the problem on Floor 2: namely, the Cambion Mizzark.

{@b Condition 2}: The party explores floor 3 with her. She has been curious about what is up there for centuries, and recently there has been a lot of noise coming from it. She does not want to explore it alone.

If the party promises the above, she will operate the elevator so that the party can explore floors 2 and 3. Once floors 2 and 3 are explored, she will take the party to the 4th floor and give them control of the Sanctum.

entries.name: Beliefs and Motivations
entries: Tarot has certain dispositions towards the other NPCs and the party both in and out of the Sanctum of Ill Omens. Tarot used the Nostradam Mirror one day out of curiosity. The mirror gave her various visions of the future, which shape her beliefs and motivations.

entries.name: Attitude Towards Giselle and The Party
entries: Tarot loves her master, and will do anything it takes to find out where she is. The Nostradam Mirror revealed to her that if she assists a party of adventurers, it will lead to the eventual release of Giselle.
Once Giselle returns, she will accomplish her {@b Great Work}, aka {@b The Ascension Ritual}. This ritual will cut off the power of the gods, thus freeing mortals from their whims.
Should Giselle be accompanying the party within her Gem Prison, they will have a heartfelt (if telepathic) reunion.

entries.name: Attitude towards Mizzark
entries: {@i Mizzark} terrifies Tarot for a multitude of reasons. The first is that his presence has always unsettled her, so much that she stopped visiting the 2nd floor. Mizzark charmed her in the past, almost to the point of her letting him go.
However, she denied his request, and the Cambion's true nature showed itself. The cruel threats he spoke terrified her even moreso.

On top of it all, The Nostradam Mirror also revealed that Mizzark will be the death of her, which is why she is adamant about the party getting him out of the tower. She will not release him by herself and is overjoyed if the adventuring party agrees to release him while she accompanies them.

entries.name: Attitude Towards Clones and Errant Clone
entries: Tarot will be shocked and surprised at the existence of the clones. However, they will also give her hope. With her limited knowledge on how the Clone spell works, Tarot will surmise that Giselle is not dead because her soul would have inhabited a clone.

The Errant Clone is another matter - Tarot will be shocked and upset that her master's work has been hijacked by an errant soul that does not belong within the clone. Since there are multiple clones, she can forgive the "transgression."
Should the party attempt to harm or otherwise disturb the clones, she will attack the party. Should the party get attacked by the Errant Clone, she will assist the party in the battle against it.

entries.name: Elevator Rune
entries: Tarot is in possession of the Elevator Rune. It acts as a key that allows the elevator to work.

entries.name: Magic Elevator
entries: {@i In the corner lies an intriguing magical elevator, a well-made blend of craftsmanship and magic. Swirling green energy lines the floor, and the elevator lights up and hums when approached. The smooth metal doors open when one stands nearby. Green lines of magical energy emanate throughout the structure. Purple fabric and dark wood decorate the interior, which feels more spacious than it actually is. Soft, ambient light shines from magical devices in the corners.}
{@i On the outside of the elevator lies an empty, rune-shaped socket.}
Inside of the elevator are four buttons marking floors 1, 2, 3, and 4. Operating the Elevator requires the {@b Elevator Rune}. Otherwise, the buttons do not work when pressed.
The elevator's magic can be circumvented, allowing operation without the Elevator Rune on a successful Arcana check of DC 20. Doing so without consulting Tarot will result in her attacking the party.

entries.name: Library
entries: {@i Towering bookshelves reach from floor to ceiling. The rich, dark wood looks well taken care of. The smell of aged parchment and incense reaches your nose, creating a feeling of peace and serenity.}

The library consists of bookshelves containing various books, encyclopedias, and educational material. However, the majority of these books are works of fiction as Giselle used this library for recreational reading. When searched, the bookshelves contain the following notable works:

items: Longsword of Lust by Theresa Dominica
Erotic Poetry Anthology by Various Artists
Beyond The Veil - Magical Mysteries Compendium

entries: Each book is worth 250gp, and Tarot will allow the party to take them as she has "already read them a hundred times."

entries.name: Tarot's Desk
entries: A plush purple chair accentuates a green work desk. On top lies a notebook detailing Tarot's daily "to do" list. Various chores make up the list, but written in the margins is a “personal to do” list. “Reread Longsword of Lust” is the most prominent entry.

Also within the desk lies a completed copy of a book titled "The Devilish Prisoner - a Tale of Forbidden Romance" by Tarot Fatebound. Tarot will blush and say that she has no idea what the book is about or who wrote it.

entries.name: Guest Book
entries: Atop Tarot's desk is a guest book and various documents and writings from the tower's owner, Giselle the Fatebound. The guest book has the following entries from hundreds of years ago:

items: "Love the decor! You've really made this place home!" - Hoopdy
"You're out of cake. Just kidding, you're my favorite" - Lady M
"The mirror didn't tell me anything." -Bartholomew Barsavi
"The couch was so comfortable! But who turned out the lights?" - Granny Ba'khan

entries.name: Reading Desk
entries: {@i There is nothing special about this desk other than half a page of Giselle's unfinished novel,} "{@i Enemies by Fate, Lovers by Choice.}"

entries.name: Rest Area
entries: {@i Two luxurious couches fill this area, filled with various cushions and blankets. A coffee table sits next to the couches. It appears that this is where Giselle would relax with a book or entertain guests. One of the cushions looks extra comfortable.}
Within the rest area is the {@item Cushion of Sleepy Time|LPPDiDSoIO}.

entries.title: Map of the Second Floor - Summoning Room with Details
entries.altText: Map of the Second Floor - Summoning Room with Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Second Floor - Summoning Room with Details

entries.title: Map of the Second Floor - Summoning Room without Details
entries.altText: Map of the Second Floor - Summoning Room without Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Second Floor - Summoning Room without Details

entries.name: Description
entries: {@i This floor is dimly lit, and shadows seem to flicker and dance on their own. The air is thick with the smell of sulfur and the residue of summoning rituals. In the middle of the floor lies a summoning circle crafted from an elaborate mosaic of intricate runes laid out in geometric patterns. Armored clay golems stand vigilant over magical cells. There is a stirring from within these cells, revealing various creatures held inside. Alarmed at your presence, some of these creatures begin pacing and snarling.}
{@i Next to the cells is a series of switches, each labeled with a number between 1 and 5. You surmise that flicking a switch will open the door to the corresponding cell.}

Centuries ago, Giselle The Fatebound would summon demons, fey, celestials, elementals, and devils into this floor of her tower using the summoning circle within. The circle in the center was protected by the {@spell Magic Circle|PHB} spell, which kept the summoned creature inside the circle long enough that she could bind them to her will with {@i Planar Binding}.

Her reasons for summoning these creatures is varied - some, she used for information. Others, she bound to use as a fighting force to protect her on her adventures. She kept some of them imprisoned on this floor in case the {@spell Planar Binding|PHB} spell wore off in order to re-bind or for interrogation. Since her absence, these creatures have been trapped for centuries and long to escape.

entries.name: Jail Cells
entries: Each cell hums with magical energy. Each cell is a cage made from magical 1/2-inch diameter bars spaced 1/2 inch apart, similar to the prison created by the {@spell Forcecage|PHB} spell. Creatures inside of the cell cannot leave it by non magical means.

Teleportation or Interplanar travel attempts to leave the cage require a DC 32 charisma saving throw. On a success, the creature can use that magic to exit the cage. On a failure, the creature can't exit the cage and wastes the use of the spell or effect. The cage also extends into the Ethereal Plane, blocking ethereal travel.

Spells dissipate when they hit the walls of the cell, causing spells to be unable to enter or exit the cage. Spells cast outside of the cell cannot enter either as they fizzle out the same way. Only air can enter or exit the cell.
The cells can be opened by the levers nearby. Each cell is labeled 1-5, and the levers are also marked in kind.

entries.name: Imprisoned Creatures
entries: Below are a few of the creatures that can be imprisoned within the Summoning Floor. Their bindings to Giselle have long expired.
The most important one is Mizzark the Cambion, who will greet the party upon arrival.
For the other prisoners, roll a 1d6 on the table below to fill out the rest of the cells.
If released, each imprisoned creature will have their own agenda. They may attack, assist Mizzark, or try to escape.

entries.name: Mizzark The Cambion
entries: {@i Standing at nearly 8 feet tall, Mizzark boasts a slim but muscular physique. His crimson-colored skin complements his well-tailored black and gold doublet. Two horns curl from his head of jet black, well-groomed hair. How he has managed to keep up these appearances despite being imprisoned for so long is a mystery.}

Like the other creatures on this floor, Mizzark has been imprisoned for centuries. As a charming devil, he will greet the party and Tarot (if present) enthusiastically, using every trick in the book to get them to like him and feel for his plight. He will ask the party to release him and even go so far as to make a deal for his release. Like all Devilish contracts, however, there is a catch.

While calm and collected at first, Mizzark will be angered should the party refuse to free him. He knows his options for escape are limited and will not push too hard to make a contract too heavily in his favor if it hurts his chances of escape. Manipulative to the last, he will do or say whatever it takes to get released, even egging on a fight with a party member if it means he can get out of the cell.
Below are some of the offers he can make:

items: {@b Warlock Patron:} Mizzark offers fiendish powers and offers to become a group member's warlock patron. Use {@i Pact of the Fiend} from the PHP for this.
{@b Wealth:} An enormous sum of money is always up for grabs.
{@b Power and Strength:} For a warrior or fighter of the group, Mizzark offers a powerful weapon forged in the hells. It is potentially cursed.

{@b Knowledge and Wisdom:} Mizzark offers access to spells, secrets, or lore that a wizard or scholar would be after. Mizzark may also know an important piece of plot information relevant to your campaign.
{@b Personal Desires:} Mizzark can offer to reunite a party member with a lost loved one, cure an illness, or offer vengeance for a past wrong. Nothing is truly off the table.
{@b Future Favor:} Mizzark will offer to help the party in the future with a favor that they ask.

entries.name: Releasing Mizzark
entries: If released, Mizzark will hold up his end of any deals made and then use {@spell Teleport|PHB} or {@spell Plane Shift|PHB} to leave the tower. If the party has challenged him to a fight or attempts to kill him, he will attempt to release the other prisoners to fight the party. The Guardian Golems will assist the party if Tarot is still alive and with the party.
Should the party refuse to release Mizzark, he will eventually escape and hunt down the party for vengeance.

entries.name: Mizzark and Tarot
entries: Mizzark attempted to use his charm on Tarot in order for her to release him. He was almost successful as well -- Tarot may have developed feelings for the Cambion (or rather, what her understanding of love is as a magical construct that has been reading too many of Giselle's romance novels).

When Tarot denied his request to be freed, Mizzark lashed out with anger, terrifying Tarot. He will try to charm her once again should she be accompanying the party and tease her about their past conversations.

entries.name: Mizzark and Giselle
entries: Giselle the Fatebound finds it hilarious that Mizzark is still captured and hasn't found a way out of the cells and will actively antagonize Mizzark. However, Mizzark will have barbs of his own and comment that Giselle is in a prison of her own and that she should not throw stones.

Mizzark has knowledge about Giselle's part in the {@b Ascension Ritual}. He may threaten to reveal that information to the party unless Giselle or Tarot agrees to release him. Both Tarot and Giselle will want to keep this secret and will agree that he needs to be released.

entries.name: Summoning Circle
entries: {@i A circle of intricate runes lines the floor, arrayed in various geometric patterns. Aged candles and other occult paraphernalia take up spaces in and around the runes.}

The summoning circle was created by Giselle to make the summoning and binding of powerful creatures easier. The runes have been modified in a way that amplifies the effects of the {@spell Magic Circle|PHB} spell and amplifies the effects of {@spell Planar Binding|PHB}.
{@b DM NOTE}: Daring parties may attempt to bind some of the prisoners and even Mizzark himself.
An Arcana Check DC 10 will reveal the abilities of the Summoning Circle Below. Alternatively, Tarot or Giselle will explain these benefits if they are accompanying the party.

items: When {@spell Magic Circle|PHB} is cast upon this summoning circle, the duration is 24 hours instead of 1. When casting {@spell Magic Circle|PHB} using a 4th level slot or higher, the duration increases by 10 hours instead of 1 hour for each slot level above 3rd.
When a creature tries to use teleportation or interplanar travel to enter or exit the cylinder, it suffers -3 to their Charisma saving throw in addition to the other effects of Magic Circle.
Creatures targeted by {@spell Planar Binding|PHB} while inside of the Summoning Circle suffer an extra -3 to their Charisma saving throws to resist the effects of Planar Binding.
The duration of creatures bound by Planar Binding is 7 days instead of 24 hours. If cast using higher spell slots, the duration is increased by the following:

items:: 6th level spell slot: increases from 10 days to 30 days
7th level spell slot: increases from 30 days to 180 days
8th level spell slot: increases from 180 days to 365 days
9th level spell slot: increases from 365 days to 730 days

entries.name: Guardian Golems
entries: Two clay golems stand at attention. They have features similar to Tarot, but are clad in full plate armor and wielding halberds. Upon further investigation, these golem jailers are revealed to be clay golems of the same make as those on the bridge.

entries.name: If approached by the party
entries: The clay golems come to life, heralded by a loud hum as the magic within amplifies.

If Tarot is not with the party, the golems will attack. Should any prisoners get out of their cells, the Golems will attempt to place them back into the cells unless told to stand down by Tarot or Giselle.
These golems use the same stat block as those found on the bridge.

entries.title: Map of the Third Floor - Cloning Chamber with Details
entries.altText: Map of the Third Floor - Cloning Chamber with Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Third Floor - Cloning Chamber with Details

entries.title: Map of the Third Floor - Cloning Chamber without Details
entries.altText: Map of the Third Floor - Cloning Chamber without Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Third Floor - Cloning Chamber without Details

entries.name: Description
entries: {@i Bright lights shine throughout this room, illuminating the space within. A circular pattern of runes lies on the floor, similar yet very different to the one on the previous floor. Large tanks fill the west side of the room, consisting of what appears to be glass reinforced by metal.}
{@i The water within these tanks is a milky, murky white. Within each tank floats the blurred outline of a beautiful human female. One of the tanks is destroyed, shattered glass upon the floor.}
{@i In the corner, you hear a voice.} "{@i who am I?}"}

entries.name: Errant Clone
entries: {@b Dm Note}: {@i This encounter fits in nicely with the hook, {@b The Soul Of A Loved One}, and can be tied closely to the party in this way. It can also be where a recently deceased party member ended up.}
This clone of Giselle Fatebound malfunctioned not too long ago, and has been distraught on this floor ever since.

The soul of another ended up within this clone upon its death. However, the clone possesses memories and a fraction of the spellcasting abilities of the original Giselle Fatebound alongside the memories of the person whose soul now inhabits the clone.

It knows that it is a clone, as evidenced by the remaining clones within the chamber. It has been trapped here out of fear despite knowing how to use the {@spell Teleportation|PHB} Circle on the floor.
The Errant Clone's time trapped on this floor and staring at the other clones has caused it immeasurable mental anguish.
The Errant Clone may attack the party if they push her too hard.

entries.name: Errant Clone Attitude Towards Tarot
entries: The Errant Clone will beg Tarot for answers as to what and who she is, which Tarot will answer. While disappointed that the clone is not Giselle herself, Tarot is excited to see that the other clones are unused, meaning that Giselle is alive somewhere.

entries.name: Errant Clone Attitude Towards Giselle
entries: The Errant Clone will be distraught once it (telepathically) meets the person it is a clone of and beg to be released into a new body.

entries.name: Clone Tanks
entries: {@i Hexagonal glass tanks supported by reinforced metal beams stand tall on this floor. The milky-white substance filling each tank swirls serenely. Floating within each tank is the body of a female human - identical to all of the other bodies within the tanks.}

The clone tanks were created hundreds of years ago. They are extremely sturdy, durable, and were never meant to be found, not even by Giselle's clay golem servant Tarot. These tanks protect Giselle's most prized asset - her clones, which serve as a measure against death and a form of immortality. Instead of going the path of the Lich like many other wizards, Giselle utilizes the {@spell Clone|PHB} spell to create copies of herself.

entries.name: Clones
entries: These clones are identical copies of the wizard Giselle Fatebound as created by the {@spell Clone|PHB} spell. Each clone has fully matured and is awaiting the soul of Giselle Fatebound to fill it upon her death. One can surmise that Giselle has not died because her soul has not taken over any of the clones.
Tarot will warn the party not to disturb the clones or the clone tanks. Doing so will cause her to attack the party.

entries.name: Teleportation Circle
entries: A 10-foot diameter circle lies on the ground, inscribed with various sigils and runes.

{@b A DC 14 Arcana Check} reveals that this is a {@spell Teleportation Circle|PHB} as created by the {@spell Teleportation Circle|PHB} spell. Giselle Fatebound created this permanent Teleportation Circle within her cloning room in order to easily travel in and out of the tower to check on her clones and update her spare spellbooks. While she is able to teleport her original spellbook to herself via the {@spell Drawmij's Instant Summons|PHB|Instant Summons spell}, Giselle Fatebound was always paranoid and kept backups just in case.
With the location of this {@spell Teleportation Circle|PHB} revealed to the party, any party member may attune to it and use it to teleport to this location via the {@spell Teleport|PHB} spell.

entries.name: Dressing Area
entries: This section of the room contains spare clothes for a resurrected Giselle. Well-tailored, high quality robes hang from a beautiful wooden wardrobe. A large mirror also lies in this section upon a well-cared for rug.

entries.name: Desk 1
entries: This desk contains the following spare spellbooks that Giselle kept in case resurrects into a clone and loses her original spellbook.

entries.name: Divination Spellbook by Giselle Fatebound
entries: A {@item Divination Spellbook by Giselle Fatebound|LPPDiDSoIO|spellbook with divinations spells write by Giselle Fatebound}, the following spells are written in the book:

entries.name: Conjuration Spellbook by Giselle Fatebound
entries: A {@item Conjuration Spellbook by Giselle Fatebound|LPPDiDSoIO|spellbook with conjurations spells write by Giselle Fatebound}, the following spells are written in the book:

entries.name: Abjuration Spellbook by Giselle Fatebound
entries: A {@item Abjuration Spellbook by Giselle Fatebound|LPPDiDSoIO|spellbook with abjurations spells write by Giselle Fatebound}, the following spells are written in the book:

entries.name: Desk 2
entries: This desk contains a diamond worth 1000 gp and a magical scroll containing the spell Clone.

{@b DM NOTE}: Giving your party access to this many spells at once can be a powerful tool for a party wizard or to the party in general as the pages can be used as scrolls. Please note that should a wizard want to copy these spells to their own spellbook, they will need the appropriate gold, experience, and time to do so!

entries.title: Map of the Fourth Floor - Personal Quarters with Details
entries.altText: Map of the Fourth Floor - Personal Quarters with Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Fourth Floor - Personal Quarters with Details

entries.title: Map of the Fourth Floor - Personal Quarters without Details
entries.altText: Map of the Fourth Floor - Personal Quarters without Details
entries.mapName: Map of the Fourth Floor - Personal Quarters without Details

entries.name: Description
entries: Comfort and serenity greet your senses. The smell of lavender and lilac permeates the air as you enter what looks to be the personal floor of its former occupant. A well-cared for desk lies in a corner office, filled with volumes of what looks to be research. On each wall there is a telescope to observe the night sky and possibly more.

Embedded in the floor lies an ancient artifact - a magical mirror of extreme elegance lined with crisscrossing green magical lines. A sense of hope fills you as you look into it, but those feelings of warmth soon become punctuated by dread. It is difficult to make out what you see in the mirror from a distance, but there is no doubt. This is the artifact of legend: {@b The Nostradam Mirror}.

entries.name: Artifact - The Nostradam Mirror
entries: The Nostradam Mirror was created by the great Divination Wizard, Giselle The Fatebound. With it, she could gain information, spy on people, see her enemies, watch historical events, and identify magical artifacts. She would then use that information and the mirror itself to divine potential futures.

entries.name: Telling The Future With The Mirror
entries: The Nostradam Mirror presents a fun way to introduce prophecy and omens into the adventure, which can be interpreted (or misinterpreted) in many ways.
Use this mirror to show players visions of their future within a dreamscape, to answer questions posed by the players, and reveal the information that they have been seeking.
On top of this ability, the mirror can also be used mechanically as described.

entries.name: Other Mirror Abilities
entries: Objects or creatures the party attempts to scry on that are protected by the spell {@spell Nystul's Magic Aura|PHB|Mystic Aura} are immune to the mirror's effects.
The mirror cannot be removed from the tower by any means short of a {@spell Wish|PHB} spell.

For mechanical purposes or if you need a reference to some of the mirror's abilities, the mirror can be used by a creature to cast the following spells with the following modifications (if any) without the need for spell components or spell slots.

items: {@spell Scrying|PHB} - The mirror casts scrying with either no save allowed by the target or with a DC of +10
{@spell Identify|PHB} - Can be used on items that are being scried upon

entries.name: Bedroom
entries: {@i A four-post bed lies in the middle, flanked by two treasure chests that also serve as nightstands.  A section of reagents lie on shelves in the corner in what looks to be an alchemist's workshop.}

entries.name: Bed
entries: This bed is finely crafted. The mattress is extremely comfortable and soft, molding itself to the body that lies within through magic. The pillows behave the same and always seem to have the cool side up. Within the bed lies one stuffed animal - that of a round monster with purple skin, red eyes, and a giant grin.

entries.name: Treasure Chests
entries: These chests are locked and also under the effect of the {@spell Arcane Lock|PHB} spell. The passphrase to open these locks is the same as the bridge - "Future." Saying this {@b passphrase} will undo the locks and open the chests. Dispelling the {@spell Arcane Lock|PHB} spell will lower the DC of the locks on the chests by 10.
The locks on the chests require a DC 25 Thieves Tools check to unlock.
Within the chests are the following items:

items: {@item Amulet of Proof against Detection and Location|DMG}

entries.name: Personal Desk
entries: The personal desk of Giselle Fatebound. A top the desk is her diary, which contains the following entries.

entries.name: Telling the Future
entries: {@i This entry details more maddening and disjointed thoughts about prophecy from Giselle the Fatebound. The ideas both make sense but contradict one another, but the main talking point states that by seeing potential futures, you can eliminate the ones you don't like until only the ones you do are available.}

entries.name: The Mistake Of The Divine
entries: {@i This entry disparages the gods and their hold on mortals, especially with how often they interfere in the world.} "{@i Flawed creatures should not have this much power}" {@i it states. The rest of the entry details something called {@b The Ascension Ritual}, an idea her cabal has been throwing around for some time.}

entries.name: The Ascension Ritual
entries: {@i This entry is also maddening in its prose, mostly consisting of formulae and data as opposed to words. At the end of the entry it is written that} "{@i once this is complete, we can be the masters of our own destiny. The gods will never hold sway over us again.}"

entries.name: The Ascension Ritual Will Fail
entries: {@i This entry is written with a tone of frustration. It details how the cabal has deduced the incorrect formulas for the spell, and that even the smallest mistake while trying to rewrite reality could have catastrophic and apocalyptic consequences. An incorrect calculation for something like gravity could spell disaster for the world. She has warned the cabal not to attempt the ritual, but it appears they will try regardless.}

entries.name: The Gem Sanctuary
entries: {@i This entry details the creation of a Gem Sanctuary, which will protect herself from the consequences of the Ascension Ritual once it fails. The gem will be immune to most magic, both arcane and divine. However, she is worried she will not have enough time to figure out how to escape the Gem Sanctuary, and that it may become a prison. However, she has foreseen her eventual escape from this Gem and will} "{@i cross that bridge when I get to it.}"

entries.name: Balcony
entries: The balcony allows the players to view the demiplane from up high. It is a 50 foot fall to the bridge should one fall off the balcony.

entries.name: Magic Telescopes
entries: The telescopes in the adjoining towers allow the players to see the sky of the demiplane, which boasts various constellations. These magical telescopes can also peer into other planes in a similar fashion to the Nostradam mirror. These telescopes mimic the effects of the Scrying spell when used, but with the ability to see to other planes.

item.name: Cushion Of Sleepy Time
entries: Up to three creatures can share the cushion. When done so, all three fall into a wondrous 10-minute nap. At the end of the nap, if uninterrupted, all three creatures gain the effects of a Short Rest.
This item has one charge that resets every day at dawn.
Up to three creatures can share the cushion. When done so, all three fall into a wondrous 10-minute nap. At the end of the nap, if uninterrupted, all three creatures gain the effects of a Short Rest.
This item has one charge that resets every day at dawn.

entries: A spellbook with divinations spells write by Giselle Fatebound, the following spells are written in the book:

entries: A spellbook with abjurations spells write by Giselle Fatebound, the following spells are written in the book:
A spellbook with abjurations spells write by Giselle Fatebound, the following spells are written in the book:

monster.name: Guardian Golem (Difficulty Low)
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't  speak

trait.name: Acid Absorption
entries: Whenever the golem is subjected to acid damage, it takes no damage and instead regains a number of hit points equal to the acid damage dealt.

trait.name: Berserk
entries: Whenever the golem starts its turn with 20 hit points or fewer, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 6, the golem goes berserk. On each of its turns while berserk, the golem attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the golem attacks an object, with preference for an object smaller than itself. Once the golem goes berserk, it continues to do so until it is destroyed or regains all its hit points.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem's weapon attacks are magical.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes two Golem Halberd attacks.

action.name: Golem Halberd
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) slashing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Strength saving throw or be moved 5 feet to an unoccupied space, provided the target is no more than one size larger than you.

action.name: Haste {@recharge 5}
entries: Until the end of its next turn, the golem magically gains a +2 bonus to its AC, has advantage on Dexterity saving throws, and can use its slam attack as a bonus action.
Guardian Golem (Difficulty Low)

monster.name: Guardian Golem (Difficulty Low Extra Challenge)
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't  speak

trait.name: Acid Absorption
entries: Whenever the golem is subjected to acid damage, it takes no damage and instead regains a number of hit points equal to the acid damage dealt.

trait.name: Berserk
entries: Whenever the golem starts its turn with 20 hit points or fewer, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 6, the golem goes berserk. On each of its turns while berserk, the golem attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the golem attacks an object, with preference for an object smaller than itself. Once the golem goes berserk, it continues to do so until it is destroyed or regains all its hit points.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem's weapon attacks are magical.

items: When you hit a creature with an opportunity attack, the creature's speed becomes 0 for the rest of the turn. . Creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you even if they take the Disengage action before leaving your reach.

When a creature within 5 feet of you makes an attack against a target other than you (and that target doesn't have this feat), you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against the attacking creature.

When you take the Attack action and attack with only a glaive, halberd, quarterstaff, or spear, you can use a bonus action to make a melee attack with the opposite end of the weapon. This attack uses the same ability modifier as the primary attack. The weapon's damage die for this attack is a {@dice d4}, and it deals bludgeoning damage.
While you are wielding a glaive, halberd, pike, quarterstaff, or spear, other creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they enter the reach you have with that weapon.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes two Golem Halberd attacks.

action.name: Golem Halberd
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) slashing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Strength saving throw or be moved 5 feet to an unoccupied space, provided the target is no more than one size larger than you.

action.name: Haste {@recharge 5}
entries: Until the end of its next turn, the golem magically gains a +2 bonus to its AC, has advantage on Dexterity saving throws, and can use its slam attack as a bonus action.
Guardian Golem (Difficulty Low Extra Challenge)

This attack uses the same ability modifier as the primary attack. The weapon's damage die for this attack is a {@dice d4}, and it deals bludgeoning damage.
While you are wielding a glaive, halberd, pike, quarterstaff, or spear, other creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they enter the reach you have with that weapon.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The golem makes two Golem Halberd attacks.

action.name: Golem Halberd
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) slashing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Strength saving throw or be moved 5 feet to an unoccupied space, provided the target is no more than one size larger than you.

action.name: Haste {@recharge 5}
entries: Until the end of its next turn, the golem magically gains a +2 bonus to its AC, has advantage on Dexterity saving throws, and can use its slam attack as a bonus action.
Guardian Golem (Difficulty High Extra Challenge)

monster.name: Tarot, the Tower Keeper
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Acid Absorption
entries: Whenever the golem is subjected to acid damage, it takes no damage and instead regains a number of hit points equal to the acid damage dealt.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The golem is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The golem has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The golem's weapon attacks are magical.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Tarot is a 9th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is intelligence (spell save {@dc 14}, +6 to hit with spell attacks). Tarot has the following wizard spells prepared:

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Tarot makes two slam attacks

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have its hit point maximum reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken. The target dies if this attack reduces its hit point maximum to 0. The reduction lasts until removed by the greater restoration spell or other magic.

action.name: Haste {@recharge 5}
entries: Until the end of its next turn, the golem magically gains a +2 bonus to its AC, has advantage on Dexterity saving throws, and can cast a spell as a bonus action.
Tarot, the Tower Keeper

trait.name: Telepathic Bond
entries: The fiend ignores the range restriction on its telepathy when communicating with a creature it has {@condition charmed}. The two don't even need to be on the same plane of existence.

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: Mizzark's innate spellcasting ability is  Charisma (spell save {@dc 14}, +6 to hit with spell attacks). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components:

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Mizzark makes two melee attacks or uses its Fire Ray twice.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) slashing damage, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) fire damage.

action.name: Fiendish Charm
entries: One humanoid the fiend can see within 30 feet of it must succeed on a {@dc 14} Wisdom saving throw or be magically {@condition charmed} for 1 day. The {@condition charmed} target obeys Mizzark's verbal or telepathic commands. If the target suffers any harm or receives a suicidal command, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. If the target successfully saves against the effect, or if the effect on it ends, the target is immune to Fiendish Charm for the next 24 hours. Mizzark can have only one target {@condition charmed} at a time. If it charms another, the effect on the previous target ends.

action.name: Fire Ray
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 7} to hit, range 120 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) fire damage.
Mizzark,the Cambion

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The Errant Clone has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Errant Clone is a 15th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is intelligence (spell save {@dc 17}, +9 to hit with spell attacks). The Errant Clone can cast invisibility at will and has the following wizard spells prepared:
footerEntries: * The errant clone casts these spells on itself before combat.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage in melee, or 4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) piercing damage at range.

reaction.name: Portent (3/day)
entries: When the Errant Gone or a creature it can see makes an attack roll, saving throw, or an ability check, Errant Clone rolls a {@dice d20} and chooses whether to use that roll in place of the {@dice d20} rolled for the attack roll, saving throw, or ability check.
Errant Clone
